title: AI Copilots in Real Estate: Evidence from China
study_design_description: The study employs a difference-in-differences (DiD) design exploiting cross-district variation in pre-existing app penetration, estimated using a continuous diff-in-diffs regression with a flexible event-study specification to test for parallel pre-trends. This is complemented by a field experiment in Jinan that randomized AI copilot access at the neighborhood level, with half (34) of 68 neighborhoods assigned to control and half to treatment. The experiment ran for roughly eleven months through the end of 2019, with the AI copilot introduced on 16 January 2019.
primary_task_description: The AI copilot is embedded in the agent-client chat workflow and advises agents on four tasks: for buyers, it recommends suitable properties based on conversation text and browsing histories, and suggests professional reply scripts to improve communication; for sellers, it suggests dynamic price adjustments using comparable listings and market information, and proposes optimized titles and descriptions to highlight key selling points.
population_description: The main analysis uses a proprietary dataset from 18 cities covering roughly one year before and after the copilot's introduction, including about 230,000 completed transactions with observed final prices and time on market. A subset of 90,000 transactions are rated, with 75,596 buyer ratings and 20,336 seller ratings. The data contains a weekly panel of over 13 million listings. The field experiment in Jinan involved 68 neighborhoods total.

arms.description: In treated neighborhoods, the copilot was available in the agent app. Treatment intensity in the DiD is based on pre-treatment app penetration at the district level (share of transactions conducted through the app).
In control neighborhoods, the copilot was disabled (agents were told the feature was under maintenance).

outcomes.outcome_description: An indicator for whether the listed property is sold within three months of listing. Chosen because 54% of properties that are eventually sold are sold within 3 months.
Natural logarithm of one plus days on market for sellers
Natural logarithm of one plus days on market for buyers
Post-transaction overall rating from buyers, comprising 5-point overall ratings to evaluate agent's service
Post-transaction overall rating from sellers, comprising 5-point overall ratings to evaluate agent's service
Structured feedback label evaluating whether agent provided accurate recommendations
Structured feedback label evaluating talk inefficiency
Structured feedback label evaluating talk comfort
Structured feedback label evaluating whether pricing was optimal
Structured feedback label evaluating accuracy of selling-point descriptions

reported_statistics.effect_in_outcome_units: 18.8 percentage points increase at mean pre-treatment app penetration (0.446)
reported_statistics.verbatim_quote: for a district at mean pre-treatment app penetration (0.446), the likelihood that a listed property is sold within the first three months after it is listed increases by 18.8 percentage points (0.4215*0.446) compared to the control group (agents without access to AI).

reported_statistics.effect_in_outcome_units: 0.198 log-point decrease (5.27% decrease relative to baseline mean of 3.757)
reported_statistics.verbatim_quote: the time on market for sellers is lower by 0.198 (−0.4434*0.446) compared to the control group. Relative to a baseline pre-treatment mean of 3.757, this represents a 5.27% decrease.

reported_statistics.effect_in_outcome_units: 0.111 log-point decrease (4.04% decrease relative to baseline mean of 2.747)
reported_statistics.verbatim_quote: access to AI copilots reduces the time on market for buyers by 0.111 (-0.2484*0.446), or by 4.04% relative to the baseline mean of 2.747.

notes: This is a difference-in-differences study of an AI copilot tool introduced on a Chinese real estate platform on 16 January 2019. The main DiD exploits cross-district variation in pre-treatment app penetration (continuous treatment intensity). A complementary randomized field experiment in Jinan randomized AI access at the neighborhood level (34 treated, 34 control neighborhoods). The paper decomposes effects into buyer-facing and seller-facing workflows and examines heterogeneity by agent tenure. Key findings: AI access increases probability of sale within 3 months, reduces time-on-market for both buyers and sellers, increases transaction prices (driven by buyer-side exposure), and improves satisfaction ratings. Effects are larger for less-experienced agents. The paper notes that AI recommendations are advisory and visible only to agents, who retain full discretion. The post-period covers about one year, so longer-run equilibrium effects are not captured. Adoption rate by late 2019 was about 60% of AI recommendations. The paper reports that adoption led to an average price increase of about 17,160 RMB (approximately 2,400 USD) per transaction.
